productIdea: A sales development workspace that helps SDRs capture leads, plan outreach sequences, track qualification signals, and hand off qualified leads to account executives with clear context.
targetAudience: SDRs, sales managers, account executives, and sales operations staff.
problemStatement: SDRs often lack clear qualification criteria, follow-up sequences are inconsistent, and handoffs to AEs lose critical context about lead quality and next steps.
constraints: Keep the first release focused on lead capture, sequence planning, and handoff clarity. Avoid complex CRM integration in v1. Keep the workflow simple enough for junior SDRs.
desiredOutput: A build-ready markdown workspace with phased planning, qualification criteria, sequence templates, gates, verification files, handoff context, and enough clarity for an SDR coordination tool.
mustHaveFeatures: Lead capture, sequence planning, SDR dashboard, email outreach tracking, qualification signals, rep handoff checklist, follow-up rules.
niceToHaveFeatures: CRM integration, automated scoring, call recording notes, analytics dashboard, team leaderboard.
dataAndIntegrations: Leads, sequences, outreach activities, qualification criteria, handoff records, SDR assignments.
risks: Qualification criteria may be too vague, sequences may feel robotic, handoffs may still lose context, and SDRs may skip steps if the workflow is too complex.
successMetrics: A reviewer can understand the qualification flow, sequence structure, handoff requirements, and what evidence proves an SDR is ready to advance a lead.
nonGoals: No full CRM replacement, no marketing automation, no customer support ticketing, no billing integration in v1.
timeline: Plan a realistic first release that proves the SDR workflow works before adding richer integrations or automation.
teamContext: A sales operations lead uses Xelera Method to prepare the SDR module plan and then hands the workspace to Codex, Claude Code, OpenCode, or a development team for phased implementation.

questionnaireAnswers.north-star: The first release must prove that an SDR can capture a lead, follow a sequence, record qualification signals, and hand off to an AE with clear context.
questionnaireAnswers.primary-workflow: An SDR captures a lead from a source, selects a sequence, sends outreach, records responses, updates qualification signals, and hands off qualified leads to an AE with a summary of interactions and next steps.
questionnaireAnswers.scope-cut: Keep lead capture, sequence planning, outreach tracking, qualification signals, and handoff context in v1. Defer CRM integration, automated scoring, and advanced analytics.
questionnaireAnswers.acceptance: A reviewer should be able to trace a lead from capture through qualification to handoff and understand what evidence proves each stage is complete.
questionnaireAnswers.operating-risks: The biggest risks are vague qualification criteria, inconsistent sequence follow-through, lost handoff context, and SDRs skipping steps.
questionnaireAnswers.user-segments: Primary users are SDRs. Secondary users are sales managers reviewing pipeline and AEs receiving handoffs.
questionnaireAnswers.stakeholder-workflow: Sales ops defines sequences and criteria. SDRs execute outreach. Managers review pipeline. AEs receive qualified leads with context.
